author: @philipvernejules9926
text: ......and it's now the pandemic of 2020 . Hospitals no longer have brass and copper door handles and rails . It's been acknowledged now that the copper and alloys of added an extra measure of hygiene  control . Might be an opportunity for someone to be in business wand copper electroplating the stainless and even the plastic handles n rails.

author: @peterfinkill8098
text:
Hi. I cannot quite grasp what you are saying at a couple of points. Can you elucidate please.
3.50 – 3.55 – "Ok I’m wearing gloves. Because the XXXXXX that is in there Is 98% sulphuric acid. You’ll se it’s gone from this ‘straw’ yellow, towards this green kind of yellow…….." What is the word(s) at XXXXXX?
4.52 - 4.55 - "because the (lacey?) won’t plate directly onto the carbon. And that’s a shame...." Is the word lacey?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
because in there is 98%....
lacey solution is from this video

Safe Electroplating Solution For Copper and A Whole Host Of Other Metals

author: @mottabobo3217
text: Thank you for sharing this with the world for free. It's amazing and so are you.

author: @FredGandt
text:
When you say "heat exchange" could this be a method to make custom heat sinks for electronics? The usual aluminium ones aren't cheap when they're off the shelf, so being able to quickly whip up a full custom sink for whatever project is on the bench would be a real boon to makers.

at some point I intend upgrading my PC, and in the process intend to build a fully custom _case_ to house it all, and have been interested in these methods of creating custom electrical components, since I think many may come in very handy (I think I may have to buy a microwave!).
Dealing with heat generation efficiently is one of the main issues I've concerned myself with, and am hoping to create a silent, fanless case with _passive_ cooling (convection and stuff).
Big, cheap, custom heat sinks would obviously be very useful :-)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's exactly what i mean mate

author: @thagrit
text: Well that sounds like a chemists challenge if I ever heard one. Why doesn't the safer plating method plate carbon and how can we make it do that job. If those questions already have answers could you please enlighten us about them?

replies.author: @thagrit
replies.text: By the way since I didn't say it first that is amazing! I could see taking shaped sponges to hold the nickel oxide hydroxide and iron plated sponges to make a nickel iron battery.

replies.author: @thagrit
replies.text: Oh! What if you made that as a heater substrate plate the edges copper and a strip down the center then put carbon ladders between the strips?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's not a simple answer mate - normally these things are matched - it is actually astonishing that the safer method plates so much not the other way round - it is never going to do everything. I am glad you like this vid though
